SIGNIFICANT TREE DAMAGE NEAR A RESIDENCE. THE ROOF OF THE RESIDENCE WAS LIFTED AND THEN DROPPED BACK ONTO THE HOUSE. A LARGE ANTENNA THAT WAS CEMENTED INTO THE GROUND WAS BLOWN OVER. THE PRELIMINARY RATING OF THE DAMAGE ASSOCIATED WITH THE TORNADO AT THIS LOCATION IS AN EF-0. -THIS IS A DELAYED REPORT-

THE NORTH SIDE OF A LARGE THREE CAR GARAGE WAS DESTROYED. DEBRIS WERE BLOWN APPROXIMATELY 100 YARDS AWAY. THE PRELIMINARY RATING OF THE DAMAGE ASSOCIATED WITH THE TORNADO AT THIS LOCATION IS AN EF-0. -THIS IS A DELAYED REPORT-

SEVERAL PRELIMINARY REPORTS OF WINDS DAMAGE ACROSS NORTHWEST FRANKLIN COUNTY. THE ROOF WAS BLOWN OFF OF AT LEAST ONE HOME AT THE INTERSECTION OF FLORIDA AND WOODSON. A BARN ROOF WAS ALSO REPORTED TO HAVE BLOWN OFF AT THE INTERSECTION OF STAFFORD AND FLORIDA. IT WAS REPORTED THAT A TREE FELL ON A HOUSE WEST OF OTTAWA ON LABETTE ROAD. POWER OUTAGES WERE REPORTED ACROSS THE WESTERN PART OF THE COUNTY.
NICKEL SIZE HAIL ALSO REPORTED.

AN UNOCCUPIED HOUSE TOOK SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE FROM A TORNADO. THE GARAGE AND APPROXIMATELY TWO THIRDS OF THE HOUSE WERE REMOVED FROM THE FOUNDATION. DEBRIS FROM THE HOUSE WWRE BLOWN A FEW HUNDRED YARDS AWAY. THE PRELIMINARY RATING OF THE DAMAGE ASSOCIATED WITH THE TORNADO AT THIS LOCATION IS AN EF-2. -THIS IS A DELAYED REPORT-
2 POWER POLES DOWN AT INTERSECTION OF 105TH AND STUBBS RD.

A METAL OUTBUILDING WAS COLLAPSED. DEBRIS FROM THE BUILDING WERE CARRIED A SHORT DISTANCE AWAY. THE PRELIMINARY RATING OF THE DAMAGE ASSOCIATED WITH THE TORNADO AT THIS LOCATION IS AN EF-0. -THIS IS A DELAYED REPORT-

EMERGENCY MANAGER REPORTS 6 HOMES HEAVILY DAMAGED OR DESTROYED BY A TORNADO ALONG A LINE FROM 1.5 MILES SOUTH OF COURTLAND TO 6 MILES NORTH OF BELLEVILLE.
